problem_addressed: How to effectively select informative samples in open-set active learning when unlabeled data contains both known and unknown classes
core_claim: The proposed purity-based sampling strategy with GMM modeling achieves superior query precision and test accuracy in open-set active learning
key_contributions: Purity-based candidate selection mechanism that distinguishes between known and unknown samples
GMM modeling of purity regimes to handle different sample types (high, low, intermediate purity)
Flexible sampling strategy that maintains high query precision from early training stages
novelty: Introduces a novel purity metric and flexible sampling strategy that effectively constructs high-purity candidate sets without additional hyperparameters
method_summary: Trains a dual-head classifier with LCE and LEDL losses, estimates unknown classes, computes purity margins, models purity regimes with GMM, and selects samples based on likelihood and information scores
method_components: Dual-head classifier training (LCE for primary, LEDL for auxiliary)
Unknown class estimation using labeled pool and upper bound
Purity margin computation with/without unknown classes
key_innovation: The purity-based candidate selection mechanism that computes Spurity(x) = maxc∈Ck oc − maxc∈Cˆu oc for samples with unknown classes, enabling effective discrimination between known and unknown samples
assumptions: Labeled pool contains only known classes
Unlabeled pool may contain both known and unknown classes
Number of unknown classes can be estimated from labeled data
theoretical_grounding: The method is empirically validated through extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks, showing superior performance without additional theoretical proofs
title: Revisiting Unknowns: Towards Effective and Efficient Open-Set Active Learning
